subject: [PATCH v5 08/22] x86/virt/seamldr: Allocate and populate a module update request

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary: Reviewer pointed out that @sig being 4k aligned does not necessarily imply page size alignment, and suggested adding a comment to clarify that block->offset_of_module is relative to the start of struct tdx_blob, not blob->data.
reviews.raw_body:
Sure. How about:

		/*
		 * @sig is 4KB-aligned, but that does not imply PAGE_SIZE
		 * alignment when PAGE_SIZE != SZ_4K. Always include the
		 * in-page offset.
		 */

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch corrects an issue in the x86/virt/seamldr module where it incorrectly handles memory alignment for a signature (@sig) that is not 4k aligned. The fix involves adjusting the pointer to the next 4k boundary when handling such cases. Additionally, the patch adds a comment to clarify how block->offset_of_module is calculated relative to the start of the struct tdx_blob.
